hero.title: The agnostic, accessibility-first Vue 3 component framework
hero.lede: Guiding defaults you can fully restyle · bring your own CSS framework · native modern HTML · first-class TypeScript · MIT, forever.

home.earlyNote: Early development — the API is being designed in the open. Nothing is on npm yet.

componentsIndex.heading: Components
componentsIndex.lede: The components built so far. More land as the roadmap progresses — this list grows with them.

getting-started.tagline: Install, add a theme, and render your first component.

gettingStarted.heading: Getting started
gettingStarted.lede: Install {core}, add a theme for the CSS, and render your first component. Works the same in a plain Vue (Vite) app or in Nuxt.

callout.body: The API is being designed in the open and nothing is on npm yet — the steps below document the intended shape. Follow the {roadmap} for the first release.

install.heading: Installation
install.body: You need two packages: {core} for the components and a theme for the CSS. Core ships {no} CSS of its own — without a theme the components render, but unstyled.

vite.heading: Vue (Vite)
vite.intro: Install the plugin with {createBankai} and import the theme's CSS once at your entry point.
vite.then: Then import components where you use them:

nuxt.heading: Nuxt
nuxt.intro: Add the first-party module and the theme CSS. The module auto-registers every component (so templates use {tag} with no import), auto-imports the composables, and installs the config per app so it stays per-request under SSR.
nuxt.optionsIntro: Configure the module under the {bankai} key. Every option is optional.

theme.heading: Choosing a theme
theme.intro: Themes are separate CSS packages — core resolves against none of them, so you pick one and import its CSS. Two ship today:
theme.bankai: {pkg} — the signature house look, an opinionated set of styled defaults. Customize it through {vars} custom properties.
theme.tailwind: {pkg} — remaps the design tokens onto your Tailwind scale, so your own Tailwind design language drives the components. Requires Tailwind CSS v4.
theme.override: Both override cleanly because the theme CSS is authored with zero-specificity {where} selectors.

config.heading: Configuration
config.intro: Pass initial config to {createBankai} (or the module's {bankaiConfig}). Read or mutate it at runtime with {useBankaiConfig} inside a component's setup.

options.idGeneration: Whether components generate a stable id when the consumer supplies none. A consumer-provided id always wins.
options.warnings: Emit development warnings for likely-mistaken usage. Already stripped from production builds; set false to silence them in dev too.
options.linkComponent: Force the component BankaiLink renders for internal navigation. Leave unset to auto-detect NuxtLink → RouterLink → <a>.
options.linkOrigin: Site origin BankaiLink compares an href against to decide it is external. Set it for accurate, hydration-safe checks under SSR/SSG.
options.linkNoopener: Auto-add rel="noopener noreferrer" to a target="_blank" link. A consumer-provided rel always wins.
options.codeBlockCopiedDuration: How long (ms) BankaiCodeBlock's copy button stays in its copied state before reverting. A per-instance copiedDuration prop overrides it.

next.heading: Next steps
next.body: Browse the {components} for props, slots, and live examples.
